subject: Mathematics
title: Square and Cube Numbers
description: Learn Square and Cube Numbers in one minute.

hook.text: Arrange dots in a perfect square: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25. These are the square numbers.

buildup.text: Each square number is n times n. The gaps between them are the odd numbers: 3, 5, 7, 9.

discovery.text: Cube numbers fill 3D boxes: 1, 8, 27, 64, 125. Each is n times n times n.

twist.text: The sum of the first n odd numbers always equals n squared. A surprising visual proof uses L-shapes.

climax.text: Square numbers underpin area formulas, Pythagorean triples, and quadratic equations.

punchline.text: Squares and cubes turn geometry into numbers and back.

quiz.question: What is the pattern in the gaps between consecutive square numbers?
